text.title: Shopping Ads API Gotchas and Agent Rules
text.summary: Two-API authentication requirements, product ID format, GMC suspension cascade, currency unit differences, and agent-specific failure modes.

rule.statement: Maintain two separate service-account credential sets: Content API scope for Merchant Center operations and Google Ads API scope for campaign operations; never conflate customer_id (Ads) with merchant_id (GMC).
rule.rationale: The two APIs have different authentication scopes, rate quotas, and ID namespaces. An agent that mixes them will authenticate against the wrong service and produce confusing 401 or resource-not-found errors.

rule.statement: API quotas are separate: Content API has its own quota distinct from Google Ads API quota; bulk feed uploads can starve campaign-management calls if not rate-limited independently.
rule.rationale: A large feed upload run simultaneously with campaign setup can exhaust the Content API quota for the day, preventing further product insertions or updates until the next day's quota resets.

rule.statement: Construct full product IDs with the format "online:en:US:SKU123" (channel:language:country:item_id); using just the SKU without the prefix will fail with resource-not-found errors.
rule.rationale: Content API product IDs include channel (online/local), locale, target country, and the store-defined item ID. The bare SKU alone is not a valid Content API resource identifier.

rule.statement: Merchant Center price.value is a decimal string like "29.99" with a separate price.currency field; it is NOT micros. Do not apply Google Ads micros logic (multiply by 1,000,000) to Content API price fields.
rule.rationale: Agents working across Google Ads and Content API in the same session commonly apply the micros convention to Content API and submit prices as "29990000" — which lists products at $29 million and triggers immediate disapproval.

rule.statement: Run the Merchant Center diagnostics daily; a single critical policy issue suspends the entire account, taking down both Shopping and Performance Max simultaneously.
rule.rationale: GMC suspensions are account-wide, not product-level. One misrepresentation or policy violation immediately stops all Shopping ad serving for every campaign linked to that account until the appeal is approved (1-3 weeks).

antipattern.description: Treating feed upload as synchronous — checking product status immediately after upload.
antipattern.reason: Feed upload is async; productstatuses.get reflects the state before the upload for up to 30 minutes after insertion. Agents checking status immediately after upload will see stale disapprovals.

list.item: Title rewrites for low-impression products: human brand review required
Policy disapproval appeals: human legal review required
Target ROAS changes above 20%: human approval required
New country expansion (new feed + shipping settings): human approval required
